{{Chess Position|= |Caro-Kann Exchange Variation| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd| | |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | |pd| | | | | |= | | | |pl| | | | |= | | | |pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= |parent=[[Chess/Caro-Kann Defence|Caro-Kann]] }} = Caro-Kann Defence: Exchange Variation = White exchanges on d5, and black almost always responds with cxd5. This forms opposite-wing majorities. This usually will lead into a Panov-Botvinnik Attack, although independent lines can arise after 4.Bd3. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. 1.e4 c6 2.d4 d5 3.exd5
